naming_violations.reason: normalize-doc-names.sh flags INDEX.md as non-compliant uppercase filename. CLAUDE.md and README.md and ARCHITECTURE.md are named safety exceptions, but INDEX.md is not listed. However, this is a pre-existing project-wide convention (multiple INDEX.md files throughout the repo) and none of the changed files introduced this pattern.
naming_violations.recommendation: Pre-existing convention across the project; not a new violation in this diff.

orphaned_subagents.reason: detect-orphan-agents.sh reports this agent is not referenced by any command. Pre-existing issue; not introduced by this diff.

unreferenced_scripts.reason: detect-orphan-scripts.sh reports no functional references. Has only documentation references (INDEX.md). One-time install script; 6 days old; commit_count=1. check-file-references.sh confirms exit code 2 (archive only).
unreferenced_scripts.recommendation: Archive to scripts/install/archive/ when convenient. Not introduced by this diff.

unreferenced_scripts.reason: detect-orphan-scripts.sh reports no references. 11 days old; commit_count=1. No matching command file.
unreferenced_scripts.recommendation: Verify purpose; if one-time utility, safe to archive or delete.

duplicate_content.reason: detect-duplicate-content.sh found many groups of byte-identical files. All groups are entirely within gitignored directories (projects/, worktrees/, file-history/). No tracked-file duplicates detected.
duplicate_content.recommendation: No action required; all duplicates are in gitignored directories.

gitignore_coverage.reason: All four HARNESS_RULES from session-gitignore-propagate.sh are present: tests/generated/ (commented-out with intentional-tracking note), .claude/dev-registry/dev-*/, docs/codex/, .playwright-mcp/. No missing harness rules.
harness_rules_checked: tests/generated/ — present (intentionally tracked per test-writer contract; comment note in .gitignore)

gitignore_coverage.reason: Untracked runtime files .last-update-result.json and .update.lock are not covered by .gitignore patterns. These appear to be runtime artifacts from the npm/Claude Code update mechanism. Neither .gitignore nor any file in the changed-files list touches these patterns.
gitignore_coverage.recommendation: Add .update.lock and .last-update-result.json (or .*.lock pattern) to .gitignore to prevent accidental commits of update-mechanism runtime state.
gitignore_coverage.note: settings.json was modified in this diff but .gitignore was NOT in the changed files list; introduced_in_diff=false per default-safe rule (AC-12.1 Mechanism ii).

detect_orphan_scripts_scanner_limitation.note: detect-orphan-scripts.sh is blind to the dynamic slash-command todo loader pattern used by this project. All scripts/todo/<command>.py files are functionally referenced when a corresponding commands/<command>.md exists — the pattern `python3 scripts/todo/<command>.py` is resolved at runtime by the orchestrator workflow. The following files were flagged by the scanner but are NOT unreferenced: scripts/todo/dev-command.py (NEW in this diff), scripts/todo/dev.py (MODIFIED in this diff), scripts/todo/site-navigate.py, scripts/todo/security-check.py, scripts/todo/research-deep.py, scripts/todo/refactor.py, scripts/todo/playwright-helper.py, scripts/todo/optimize.py, scripts/todo/explain-code.py, scripts/todo/doc-gen.py, scripts/todo/deep-search.py, scripts/todo/code-review.py, scripts/todo/redev.py, scripts/todo/dev-overnight.py. Safety Rule: Do not delete or archive scripts/todo/<command>.py files when a matching commands/<command>.md exists or when the dynamic todo loader can resolve them.

local_gitignored_temp_files.note: Two temp files exist in the gitignored plugins/ directory: plugins/blocklist.json.b5acb09ac17645d7.tmp (created March 11) and plugins/blocklist.json.710b14b9f0749d21.tmp (created March 12). Both are confirmed gitignored by .gitignore line 22 (plugins/ rule). These are runtime plugin state artifacts and will never be tracked. No action required.

new_test_files_untracked.note: tests/test_graphify_scripts.py and tests/test_graphify_workflow_contract.py are new files in the changed-files list but appear as untracked (not yet staged/committed). They test existing scripts (scripts/graphify_lib.py, hooks/pretool-cp-checkin.py, scripts/spec-check.py, hooks/prompt-workflow.py) which all exist on disk. These are well-formed integration tests placed in the correct tests/ root directory following project convention. No action needed — they will be picked up in the next commit.

summary.notes: No cleanliness issues were newly introduced by this diff (introduced_in_diff: true = 0). All findings are pre-existing advisory items. Per AC-12.1 default-safe rule: pre-existing file-level findings without introduced_in_diff: true are non-blocking for CLOSE.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex identified 5 issues: (1) scripts/todo/dev-command.py is a false positive due to dynamic todo loader pattern — APPLIED removed from findings; (2) plugins/*.tmp files are out-of-scope for changed-files mode — APPLIED demoted to observations; (3) scripts/todo/*.py old scripts are false positives same dynamic loader reason — APPLIED moved to observations; (4) Safety Rule: do not delete scripts/todo/<command>.py when matching command exists — APPLIED added explicit safety note; (5) pre-existing items should not block close — APPLIED all have introduced_in_diff: false.

findings.issue: scripts/todo/dev-command.py flagged as unreferenced_scripts with introduced_in_diff: true is a false positive — it is a dynamic todo preloader resolved at runtime by the slash-command workflow
findings.proposed_fix: Delete the scripts/todo/dev-command.py entry from findings.unreferenced_scripts; add non-finding note that detect-orphan-scripts.sh is blind to dynamic slash-command todo loaders
findings.rationale: Codex confirmed it is dynamically referenced. Removed from unreferenced_scripts findings. Added to observations.detect_orphan_scripts_scanner_limitation.

findings.issue: plugins/blocklist.json.*.tmp entries are out of scope for --changed-files mode; not in changed_files_scope and are gitignored runtime state
findings.proposed_fix: Remove from findings.temp_files; move to observations.local_gitignored_temp_files with severity advisory
findings.rationale: Codex is correct — these files are gitignored and not in the diff scope. Moved to observations.

findings.issue: scripts/todo/*.py old scripts (site-navigate, security-check, etc.) are false positives — they follow the same dynamic todo loader pattern with matching commands/<command>.md files; severity major is misclassified
findings.proposed_fix: Remove from findings.unreferenced_scripts; move to observations.detect_orphan_scripts_false_positives with severity advisory and safe_to_delete: false
findings.rationale: Codex confirmed these follow the same dynamic loader pattern. All moved to observations with explicit safe_to_delete: false note.

findings.issue: Safety Rule violation: any deletion recommendation for scripts/todo/<command>.py files when matching commands/<command>.md exists would violate Safety Rules
findings.proposed_fix: Add explicit wording: do not delete scripts/todo/<command>.py files when matching command exists; treat detect-orphan-scripts.sh output for these as scanner limitation

findings.issue: Draft mixes changed-files findings with pre-existing full-repo scanner output; in --changed-files mode pre-existing items should not appear as actionable findings
findings.proposed_fix: Limit findings to changed files only; move pre-existing/out-of-scope items to observations
findings.rationale: Applied: all pre-existing items now have introduced_in_diff: false (advisory, non-blocking per spec). Kept in findings for documentation since the inspector spec requires reporting all items with introduced_in_diff tagging; the introduced_in_diff: false tag ensures close.md treats them as non-blocking per AC-2.6(b) default-safe rule.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 5 Codex findings with PROPOSED_FIX were applied: (1) dev-command.py removed from unreferenced_scripts false positive; (2) plugins/*.tmp demoted to advisory observations; (3) scripts/todo/*.py false positives moved to scanner-limitation observations with safe_to_delete: false; (4) explicit Safety Rule note added; (5) pre-existing items retained with introduced_in_diff: false for documentation but are non-blocking.
